comment_text.sn7.5:1.4: The name “harmless” (ahiṁsaka) was also given to Aṅgulimāla after his awakening (MN 86:18.35).

root_text.sn7.5:1.2: Atha kho ahiṁsakabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn7.5:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho ahiṁsakabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.5:3.1: Evaṁ vutte, ahiṁsakabhāradvājo brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn7.5:3.4: Aññataro ca panāyasmā ahiṁsakabhāradvājo arahataṁ ahosīti.

translation_text.sn7.5:1.2: Then the brahmin Bhāradvāja the Harmless went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn7.5:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said,
translation_text.sn7.5:1.4: “I am Harmless, worthy Gotama, I am Harmless!”
translation_text.sn7.5:2.1: “If you were really like your name,
translation_text.sn7.5:2.4: does no harm by way of
translation_text.sn7.5:3.1: When he had spoken, the brahmin Bhāradvāja the Harmless said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn7.5:3.4: And Venerable Bhāradvāja the Harmless became one of the perfected.
